title: How to update multiple-person column. Please, give me example for the CURL

posts.author: olgase (Olga Semenova)
posts.cookedHtml:
IN the output
“{"query":"query { boards (ids: 2211958476) {owner{ id } columns { id title type }}}"}”

I have one of the column {
“id”: “people”,
“title”: “CD”,
“type”: “multiple-person”
}
with type “multiple-person”

I need example how to write update this column [cURL Examples for API] like as examples on the (cURL Examples for API v2 :))

posts.author: rob (Roberto Tremonti)
posts.cookedHtml:
monday API docs

People

The people column represents a person(s) who is assigned items. Check out our help center to learn more about the [people column] (https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360002281539-The-People-Column). Reading the people column You can return...

posts.author: olgase (Olga Semenova)
posts.cookedHtml:
I want to receive ids

But when I requested

{“query”: “query { items_by_multiple_column_values (board_id: 2211958476, column_id:"people", column_values:["Olga Semenova", "Inna Kaminska"]) { id name }}”}

I received

{
“data”: {
“items_by_multiple_column_values”:
},
“account_id”: 5637878
}

What is wrong?

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello there @olgase!

That is weird. I just tried the exact same query with a board in my account and it worked well.

Possible issues I can think of:

  • Using a wrong board ID
  • Using a wrong column ID
  • Looking for values that are not present in the board

Can you please send an email to appsupport@monday.com with the query, response and a screen recording or a screenshot of the board you are using? Our technical team can take a look at it and let you know how to proceed.

Cheers,
Matias
